task: System Status Box Fix - GPS Data Pipeline Analysis

issues_found: Service is designed for MAVLink GPS, not standard GPSD
Currently using GPS simulation instead of real GPSD connection
No client code found to connect to actual GPSD daemon

missing_components.gpsd_client: No GPSD client library or connection code found
missing_components.data_flow: No code connecting GPSD (port 2947) to Node.js backend
missing_components.event_handlers: No GPS data update event handlers in frontend code

details: The Node.js backend has hardcoded GPS values in the /info endpoint
No GPSD client library is installed or implemented
The gps-bridge service is for MAVLink, not standard GPSD
Frontend expects real GPS data but backend never connects to GPSD

immediate_fix.1: Install node-gpsd package: npm install node-gpsd
immediate_fix.2: Create GPSD client connection in server.js
immediate_fix.3: Update /info endpoint to return real GPS data
immediate_fix.4: Add GPS update event broadcasting via WebSocket

step1.code:
const gpsd = require('node-gpsd');
const gpsClient = new gpsd.Listener();

test_results.gpsd_connectivity: PASS - GPSD running and providing data

conclusion: GPS data pipeline is broken at the Node.js backend. GPSD is functioning correctly and providing real GPS data, but the Node.js server has no client code to connect to GPSD. Instead, it returns hardcoded GPS coordinates. The fix requires implementing a GPSD client in the Node.js backend to establish the data flow from GPSD to the frontend.
